title: botrychium ramosum var. manshuricum
intro: Botrychium ramosum var. manshuricum, also known as the Manshu rautenkapnoth, is a rare species of fern found in East Asia, specifically in China and Japan. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Chinese herbalism, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, as well as possessing antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for medicinal use by drying and processing the whole plant, and it can be administered in various forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts.
